note: Structure Sync exports three content-entity types (taxonomy terms, custom blocks, menu links) into a single config object and imports them back, filling the gap left by core CMI. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, which three kinds of content does the Structure Sync module export to configuration so they can be deployed between environments?
evals.expected_output: Taxonomy terms, custom (content) blocks, and menu links — content entities that core config management does not move.

evals.prompt: Why can't I just use Drupal's core configuration export/import (drush config:export) to deploy my taxonomy terms and menu links between environments, and what module fills that gap?
evals.expected_output: Terms, custom blocks, and menu links are content entities, not configuration, so core CMI does not export them. Structure Sync serializes them into config so they can be deployed.

evals.prompt: Which config object does Structure Sync write the exported taxonomy terms, blocks, and menu links into so they travel with drush config:export?
evals.expected_output: The structure_sync.data config object (defined in config/schema/structure_sync.schema.yml), which exports/deploys like any other config.

evals.prompt: Describe the deployment flow for moving taxonomy terms from staging to production using Structure Sync.
evals.expected_output: Export terms into structure_sync.data on staging, deploy/commit that config, then run a Structure Sync import on production to recreate the terms as entities.

evals.prompt: What Drush commands does Structure Sync provide to export and import all its structure types at once on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: export:all (ea / export-all) and import:all (ia / import-all); per-type commands also exist, e.g. export-taxonomies (et), import-menus (im), export-blocks (eb).

evals.prompt: Structure Sync imports offer three styles. Name them and explain what each does to existing entities.
evals.expected_output: safe = only add missing entities (no update/delete); full = safe plus update existing (matched by UUID); force = delete all of that type then recreate from config.

evals.prompt: Where in the Drupal admin UI do I manage Structure Sync, and which permission gates it?
evals.expected_output: Admin → Structure → Structure Sync (/admin/structure/structure-sync); all screens are gated by the core 'administer site configuration' permission — the module defines no permissions of its own.

evals.prompt: If I run a Structure Sync import twice, will it create duplicate taxonomy terms? How does it match entities?
evals.expected_output: No — entities are matched by UUID, so imports are idempotent; re-running an import does not duplicate entities.
